Call-Out From formatToDbms and formatFromDbms Triggers

You can encrypt and decrypt data that is stored and retrieved from the database using the formatToDbms and formatFromDbms triggers of a field.

For a given field, the formatToDbms and formatFromDbms triggers should perform the inverse of the functionality of the other so that if both triggers are executed, the result is the same data you started with.

This example illustrates the use of the formatToDbms and formatFromDbms triggers to encrypt a string field in the database. As a simple encryption, we will use the ROT13 algorithm, which consists of shifting every letter 13 positions up in the alphabet, cycling down to ‘a’ once we go past ‘z’. Note that if you do this twice, you have the same data again, so the operation can be used for both encryption and decryption.

CAUTION: The ROT13 algorithm protects only against accidental or casual reading, and should not be used as an alternative for industry-standard encryption methods such as DES, BASE-64, or MD5.
  1. In the CONV signature (defined in Call-Out Using activate), define a new operation named ROT13, type void, literal name ROT13, and one String IN/OUT parameter. Compile the signature and update the UAR file.
  2. In the source acttest.c, described in that same section, add this C code: 
    #include <h3gl.h>
EXPORT void ROT13 (char *data)
{
char *p;
for ( p=data; *p; p++ )
{
if ( (*p >='A' && *p <='M')
|| (*p >='a' && *p <='m') )
(*p) += 13;
else
if ( (*p >='N' && *p <='Z')
|| (*p >='n' && *p <='z') )
(*p) -= 13;
}
}
  3. Compile the C source and create the shared library acttest using the command appropriate for your operating system. For more information, see 3GL Compile and Link Commands.
  4. Add the following entry to your assignment file: 
    [USER_3GL]
acttest(ROT13)

    or on non-Windows platforms:

    [USER_3GL]
acttest(USROT13CONV)
  5. Using the entity MYENT (defined in Activate Using Occurrence and Entity Parameters), we will store the DESC field encrypted into the database. To do this, in both the formatToDbms and formatFromDbms triggers in the model definition of MYENT, enter this ProcScript: 
    activate "conv".rot13(desc)
  6. Create a simple form for this entity, compile and run it, and do a ^RETRIEVE. You will see that newly stored data will be encrypted in the database or data file. Previously stored data is now being displayed in ROT13-encrypted form, because it was not encrypted when it was stored, but it is now being decrypted when it is read.

It is also possible to use perform rather than activate. The C code would then be as follows:

#include <h3gl.h>
EXPORT int ROT13 ()
{
char *p;
char buf[10];
int len;
len = ufget((unsigned char*)"DESC", (unsigned char*)buf, sizeof(buf)-1);
buf[len] = 0;
for ( p=buf; *p; p++ )
{
if ( (*p >='A' && *p <='M')
|| (*p >='a' && *p <='m') )
(*p) += 13;
else
if ( (*p >='N' && *p <='Z')
|| (*p >='n' && *p <='z') )
(*p) -= 13;
}
ufput((unsigned char*)"DESC.MYENT", (unsigned char*)buf);
return 0;
}

and the calling ProcScript would be as follows in both triggers:

perform "ROT13"
Related Links
  • trigger formatToDbms

    System trigger for encrypting data in a field when writing to the database. Use when implementing secure systems.

  • trigger formatFromDbms

    System trigger for decrypting data for a field when reading from the database. Use when implementing secure systems.

The C code would then be as follows: #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT int ROT13 () { char *p; char buf[10]; int len; len = ufget((unsigned char*)"DESC", (unsigned char*)buf, sizeof(buf)-1); buf[len] = 0; for ( p=buf; *p; p++ ) { if ( (*p >='A' && *p <='M') || (*p >='a' && *p <='m') ) (*p) += 13; else if ( (*p >='N' && *p <='Z') || (*p >='n' && *p <='z') ) (*p) -= 13; } ufput((unsigned char*)"DESC.MYENT", (unsigned char*)buf); return 0; } Copy and the calling ProcScript would be as follows in both triggers: perform "ROT13" Copy Related Links trigger formatToDbms System trigger for encrypting data in a field when writing to the database. Use when implementing secure systems. trigger formatFromDbms System trigger for decrypting data for a field when reading from the database. Use when implementing secure systems.
